channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Suspension of Suicide Capsule Applications Amidst Ongoing Investigation
item.description: Switzerland-based advocacy group, The Last Resort, suspends applications for the 'Sarco' suicide capsule amid a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WhatsApp vs. Apple Messages: Which is the better messaging app in 2024?
item.description: Messaging apps are one of the most frequently used apps on our phones. There’s probably not a day that goes by when most people don’t hear a ding alerting them to a new text. There’s also no shortage of messaging apps.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Meta unveils Movie Gen, an AI model that can generate video with sound
item.description: Facebook owner Meta announced on Friday it had built a new AI model called Movie Gen that can create realistic-seeming video and audio clips in response to user prompts, claiming it can rival tools from leading media generation startups like OpenAI and ElevenLabs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Navigating the federal AI frontier: Top priorities for incoming chief AI officers
item.description: There are ten key focus areas for current and prospective agency CAIOs to consider to ensure and maximize the benefits and minimize the risks of AI. The post Navigating the federal AI frontier: Top priorities for incoming chief AI officers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments
item.description: Tim Gribben, the commissioner of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, said the bureau refined and reframed its progress statement for the first time since 2018. The post Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: We’ve entered the AI grift era
item.description: For years, Y Combinator has been revered as a highly selective startup accelerator with a hit rate that far exceeds the law of averages. But one of its latest companies has raised a few eyebrows, and prompted questions about whether AI grifters have caught up with the innovators.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Musk to attend Trump rally at site of attempted assassination
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he plans to attend former President Trump’s rally in battleground Pennsylvania this weekend, when Trump returns to the site where he survived the first assassination attempt on his life.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation
item.description: Cybersecurity experts from the Army Software Factory, SSA and USCIS say letting developers drive security efforts drives up safety in code. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Why airlines are turning to Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi
item.description: Last week, Air France was the latest major airline to announce it will be moving its in-flight Wi-Fi to the Starlink network, offering it for free to members of its frequent flier program.
item.dc:creator: Jack Kuhr, Fast Company

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tener prisa por alcanzar el ROI de la IA puede salir caro
item.description: Muchas organizaciones han luchado por encontrar el retorno de la inversión (ROI) después de lanzar proyectos de inteligencia artificial (IA), pero existe el peligro de exigir demasiado, demasiado pronto, según la firma de investigación y asesoramiento de TI Forrester.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why OpenAI needs another $6.6 billion in VC money
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk’s friendship with Diddy examined after he posted about boy’s alleged abuse
item.description: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' mentee Aubrey O'Day and others called out Musk for trying to distance himself from allegations that the music mogul sexually abused multiple victims, including a 9-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: The bigger the better? Approaching Generative AI by size
item.description: As the Generative AI (GenAI) hype continues, we’re seeing an uptick of real-world, enterprise-grade solutions in industries from healthcare and finance, to retail and media.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OMB gives agencies new considerations when buying AI
item.description: The Office of Management and Budget told agencies in a new memo to proactively manage AI risks, promote a competitive market and manage business processes. The post OMB gives agencies new considerations when buying AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Justice Department takes action against Russian hacking group
item.description: U.S. officials have seized dozens of web domains used by Russian intelligence agents and their proxies to steal Americans’ information, the Justice Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Addressing the public sector’s penetration testing problems
item.description: While there’s no such thing as a silver bullet solution to secure the public sector, continuous pentesting addresses these issues head-on. The post Addressing the public sector’s penetration testing problems first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Why OpenAI is becoming a for-profit business
item.description: OpenAI’s plans to restructure as a for-profit business mark a significant departure from its roots as a nonprofit research lab grounded in a commitment to build artificial intelligence (AI) to “benefit humanity.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Spring AI: An AI framework for Java developers
item.description: Artificial intelligence has been something of a fiesta for programmers for the last few years, and one language—Python—has been the undeniable belle of the ball. Java and other languages have been a bit sidelined.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Docker tutorial: Get started with Docker
item.description: Containers are a lightweight way to make application workloads portable, like a virtual machine but without the overhead and bulk typically associated with VMs. With containers, you can package apps and services and move them freely between physical, virtual, or cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: It’s a wrap for the HoloLens 2 headset
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday confirmed it is no longer producing the HoloLens 2, the company’s second-generation augmented reality headset first launched five years ago at Mobile World Congress (MWC) in Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KRON4
